title: Incremental State Machines, Concurrency, Cancellation, and Ownership · Concurrent Code Workshop
summary: A protocol pairing room predicts byte, parser, state, ownership, and cleanup transitions before assembling the first explicit protocol state, event, invariant, and ownership model for incremental state machines, concurrency, cancellation, and ownership. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of explicit protocol state, event, invariant, and ownership model while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causa…
objectives: Represent protocol phase, required input, transition, emitted event, retained buffer, limit, and terminal result explicitly across fragmented reads.
Define invariants for cursor bounds, monotonic consumption, one framing choice, one response order, one close owner, and no effects before validation.
Assign one coordinated reader and ordered writer per HTTP/1.1 connection with explicit handoff for tunnels and shutdown.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Incremental State Machine
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop protocol team handles case 3327 in a fuzzing harness varying every legal input segmentation. Build deterministic Go evidence to enforce cursor, framing, reader, and terminal ownership invariants; preserve this operating constraint: no malformed framing may be normalized and forwarded to another recipient; then truncate one content octet and classify incomplete framing without connection reuse. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Represent protocol phase, required input, transition, emitted event, retained buffer, limit, and terminal result explicitly across fragmented reads.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Incremental State Machine.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for represent protocol phase, required input, transition, emitted event, retained buffer, limit, and terminal result explicitly across fragmented reads. For Commit to the mission model · Workshop Incremental State Machine, keep Incremental State Machine connected to executable Go behavior evidence.

content.text: A workshop protocol team handles case 3335 in a signed webhook receiver behind an HTTP/2 to HTTP/1.1 gateway. Build deterministic Go evidence to enforce cursor, framing, reader, and terminal ownership invariants; preserve this operating constraint: no malformed framing may be normalized and forwarded to another recipient; then force a short write and retain the exact transmitted prefix plus remaining count. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Record low-cardinality phase, framing source, bytes, duration, terminal reason, peer class, and correlation without raw secrets or attacker-controlled labels.

content.title: Fault-isolation decision
content.text: Select evidence that identifies a cause, not a patch that only hides the State Observability symptom.

options.text: Keep the plausible output and hide the assumption to avoid confusing the stakeholder. That claim does not survive the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary.
For test an acceptance boundary, run one convenient State Observability happy path, omit ownership, error, changed input, goroutine stop, and full-toolchain checks, then accept plausible output.
Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated State Observability condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model · Workshop State Observability
